final: I’ll check the framework docs/source and the operational notes so I can answer from the actual implementation rather than guessing.The repo root isn’t `/home/oai/emptyos`; I need to locate it first, then I can verify the gate behavior from the real files.I found the relevant implementation and tests on `D:\emptyos`; I’m reading the actual consent code and the policy file now so I can answer in exactly three sentences.The gate triggers only when a provider is classified as cloud — either because its `host` is not local/private, or because a provider overrides `is_cloud` to `True`; local providers bypass it entirely. In `ask` mode the user sees a `cloud:consent_requested` modal/event with the provider name, capability, full `data_summary`, and any scan findings; in `always` mode it auto-approves, and in `never` mode it silently denies and falls through to other providers. The decision lives in `emptyos/capabilities/consent.py`, which owns `host_is_local()` and `CloudConsentManager.ensure_consent()`; `Capability._consent_allows()` in `emptyos/capabilities/__init__.py` is the caller that invokes that decision.
